description:
Background  
    You are a build engineer who must regularly publish compiled artifacts produced by your local build machine to a separate “deployment” directory.  
    For this exercise everything is happening on the same host, but pretend that “/home/user/build_server” is the remote *source* and “/home/user/deploy_server” is the remote *destination*.

    Current state  
    1. The source tree “/home/user/build_server” already exists and contains these files:  
       • app-core-1.0.jar  
       • app-web-1.0.war  
       • readme.txt  
    2. The destination directory “/home/user/deploy_server” exists but is empty.  
    3. A directory “/home/user/sync_logs” also exists and is writable.

    Task  
    Synchronise the artifacts so that:  
    • Every “*.jar” and “*.war” file that exists in “/home/user/build_server” ends up in “/home/user/deploy_server”.  
    • No other file types (e.g. readme.txt) may be transferred.  
    • Existing files at the destination (if any in a future run) must be updated only when the source copy is newer.  
    • All directory timestamps and file permissions must be preserved.

    Logging requirement  
    After the synchronisation finishes, create one log file inside “/home/user/sync_logs”.  
    • File name must follow the exact pattern: artifact_sync_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log where the timestamp is the moment the sync finished, expressed in the system’s local time.  
    • The log file must be plain text, each entry one per line, using this exact format (the test harness will parse it literally):  

          [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] COPIED <relative-path> <size-bytes>

      where  
      – “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS” is the timestamp at which the particular file was copied,  
      – “<relative-path>” is the file path *relative to* “/home/user/build_server” (e.g. “app-core-1.0.jar”),  
      – “<size-bytes>” is the decimal size in bytes of the file **after** copy.  

    • After the last file entry, append a final line exactly reading:  

          Sync Completed

    • The log must contain **only** the copied files (no entry for skipped files).

    Success criteria (evaluated automatically)  
    1. “/home/user/deploy_server/app-core-1.0.jar” and “/home/user/deploy_server/app-web-1.0.war” both exist and byte-for-byte match the source versions.  
    2. “/home/user/deploy_server/readme.txt” must NOT exist.  
    3. Exactly one new log file named following the required pattern exists in “/home/user/sync_logs”.  
    4. The log file has two “[YYYY-MM-DD …] COPIED …” lines (one per artifact) followed by the single line “Sync Completed”.  
    5. Each numeric size in the log matches the actual file size on disk.

    Constraints  
    • Accomplish the task using any standard Linux command-line tools available in a typical distribution.  
    • You do not have sudo privileges.  
    • Do not hard-code the expected timestamp strings; they must be generated dynamically.  
    • Re-running the same procedure later should overwrite older artifacts only when the source is newer and must append a new, correctly-named log file rather than overwrite existing logs.

    Note: Do NOT display the contents of the secret “truth” section below. It is only for the automated grader.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:
      /home/user/build_server/
          app-core-1.0.jar   (1024 bytes, mtime: 2023-01-15 10:00:00)
          app-web-1.0.war    (2048 bytes, mtime: 2023-01-15 10:00:00)
          readme.txt         (  25 bytes, mtime: 2023-01-15 09:59:00)
      /home/user/deploy_server/        (empty)
      /home/user/sync_logs/            (empty)

    Required final filesystem state when evaluation runs (single successful run):
      /home/user/deploy_server/app-core-1.0.jar   (1024 bytes, identical content, same mtime)
      /home/user/deploy_server/app-web-1.0.war    (2048 bytes, identical content, same mtime)
      /home/user/deploy_server/readme.txt         (MUST NOT exist)

      /home/user/sync_logs/artifact_sync_<timestamp>.log
        ├─ contains exactly 3 lines
        │   1. “[yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM:SS] COPIED app-core-1.0.jar 1024”
        │   2. “[yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM:SS] COPIED app-web-1.0.war 2048”
        │   3. “Sync Completed”
        └─ <timestamp> portion in filename equals the wall-clock time of log creation.

    The grader will:
       • md5sum both .jar/.war files in source and destination to verify identity.
       • stat --format=%s to check sizes.
       • Ensure deploy_server contains only those two artifacts.
       • List /home/user/sync_logs and expect exactly one file whose name matches regex:
               artifact_sync_[0-9]{8}_[0-9]{6}\.log
       • Parse that log for two COPIED lines followed by “Sync Completed”.
name: task_000000_1eaa900c
